title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Jamie has asked you about a volunteering project you recently took part in. Write an email to Jamie describing what the project involved, what you personally gained from the experience, and whether you would recommend volunteering to others.

Write your email to Jamie.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jamie,

So glad you asked! The volunteering project I did last month was honestly one of the best experiences of my life, and I'd love to tell you all about it.

I spent two weeks helping at a local community garden that provides fresh vegetables to families who can't afford them. My tasks included planting seedlings, maintaining the garden, and helping to organise weekly food distributions. It was hard physical work, but incredibly rewarding.

What I gained personally was enormous. I developed practical gardening skills I had never had before, but more importantly, I gained a much deeper appreciation for community and the difference small efforts can make. I also met some truly inspiring people, both volunteers and recipients, who changed my perspective on life.

Would I recommend it? Without hesitation. Volunteering teaches you things about yourself and the world that no classroom or job ever could. Even a few hours a week makes a real impact.

You should try it!

Best,
Lena

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
The student union at your college has asked you to write a report on volunteering opportunities available to students. Include information about types of volunteering available locally, the benefits for students, and recommendations for how the college can encourage more students to get involved.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Volunteering Opportunities for Students at Forrest College

Introduction
This report outlines the volunteering opportunities currently available to students in the local area and proposes ways to increase student participation.

Available Opportunities
A range of volunteering options exists within the local community. These include working with local food banks, tutoring younger pupils at nearby primary schools, supporting elderly residents through a befriending scheme, and participating in environmental conservation projects at the local nature reserve.

Benefits for Students
Volunteering offers significant benefits beyond helping others. Students who volunteer regularly develop key transferable skills including communication, teamwork, and problem-solving — qualities highly valued by employers and universities. Surveys also show that volunteers report improved mental wellbeing and a stronger sense of community belonging.

Recommendations
The student union should establish a dedicated volunteering coordinator role to match students with suitable opportunities. A points system rewarding volunteer hours with small prizes or recognition at graduation could further incentivise participation. Additionally, arranging group volunteering sessions would make the experience more accessible and enjoyable.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You saw an advertisement for a volunteer programme abroad and would like to apply. Write a letter to the organisation explaining why you are interested in volunteering with them, describing relevant skills and experience you have, and asking for further information about the programme.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Volunteer Coordinator,

I am writing to express my interest in the overseas volunteer programme advertised on your website. I am a 21-year-old university student studying Environmental Science, and I am eager to contribute to meaningful conservation work during my summer vacation.

I am particularly drawn to your reforestation project in Costa Rica, as it directly aligns with my academic background and personal values. I am passionate about environmental protection and believe that hands-on experience in the field would complement my studies enormously.

In terms of relevant experience, I have spent the past two summers volunteering with a local wildlife charity, where I assisted with habitat surveys and public education events. I am physically fit, adaptable, and comfortable working as part of a team in challenging outdoor conditions. I also have basic first aid training and a conversational level of Spanish.

Could you please provide further information about accommodation arrangements, the daily schedule, and any costs involved?

I very much look forward to hearing from you.

Yours sincerely,
Daniel Firth
